hypothesis: If a single score-based diffusion model is trained exclusively on metastable-state data and, at inference, is guided by interpolated conditional scores between endpoint states and refined with Score-Aligned Ascent, then it will a priori generate transition-state configurations with committor ≈ 0.5 and near-saddle energies more accurately than linear or geodesic interpolation baselines because the score difference approximates the reaction coordinate and directs energy ascent toward the saddle while minimizing orthogonal forces.
